question_text: Read the passage shown below and identify the central theme.

options.B: The necessity of friendship in a good life
options.C: The pursuit of wealth and power

answer_key.explanation: The passage opens with 'Friendship is one of the things most necessary for life' and argues that even those with wealth and power need friends. The central theme is the necessity of friendship.
common_mistakes: Picking C because wealth and power are mentioned — they are mentioned as a counterpoint, not the theme.
answer_key.related_concept: Identifying central theme in a passage

visual.caption: Passage on friendship
visual.altText: An eight-line numbered passage adapted from Aristotle's Nicomachean Ethics on the necessity of friendship.

lines.text: Friendship is one of the things most necessary for life,
for without friends no one would choose to live,
even if he had all other goods.
Indeed, the rich and those in possession of authority and power
are thought to need friends most of all,
for what is the use of such prosperity
which is exercised chiefly and in its most laudable form towards friends?
